quotes.quote: Let us never negotiate out of fear. But let us never fear to negotiate.
quotes.author: John F. Kennedy
quotes.authorBio: Thirty-fifth president of the United States, known for speeches on diplomacy, civic responsibility, freedom, and international cooperation.
quotes.verificationNote: Verified word-for-word in the John F. Kennedy Presidential Library transcript of the January 20, 1961 inaugural address.

newsFit.headline: European leaders coordinate support for Ukraine and discuss protection, sanctions and diplomacy
newsFit.explanation: Selected for today’s focus on military support, international coordination, and the continued need for diplomacy.

quotes.quote: The only way to do great work is to love what you do.
quotes.author: Steve Jobs
quotes.authorBio: American entrepreneur and cofounder of Apple who helped shape personal computing and digital media.
quotes.verificationNote: Verified word-for-word against Stanford University’s prepared transcript of the June 12, 2005 address.

quotes.quote: Nothing great was ever achieved without enthusiasm.
quotes.author: Ralph Waldo Emerson
quotes.authorBio: American essayist, lecturer, poet, and a leading figure of transcendentalism.
quotes.verificationNote: Verified in Emerson’s essay “Circles” in the Project Gutenberg text of Essays: First Series.

quotes.quote: Success is to be measured not so much by the position that one has reached in life as by the obstacles which he has overcome.
quotes.author: Booker T. Washington
quotes.authorBio: American educator, author, orator, and a major leader of Black education in the late nineteenth and early twentieth centuries.
quotes.verificationNote: Verified in Washington’s autobiography Up from Slavery.

quotes.quote: It is impossible to live without failing at something.
quotes.author: J. K. Rowling
quotes.authorBio: British author best known for the Harry Potter series and for writing on imagination, failure, and resilience.
quotes.sourceTitle: The Fringe Benefits of Failure, and the Importance of Imagination
quotes.verificationNote: Verified as an exact sentence from Rowling’s June 5, 2008 Harvard commencement address.

quotes.quote: Diligence is the mother of good luck.
quotes.author: Benjamin Franklin
quotes.authorBio: American printer, writer, scientist, inventor, diplomat, and one of the Founding Fathers of the United States.
quotes.sourceTitle: The Way to Wealth, included with The Autobiography of Benjamin Franklin
quotes.verificationNote: Verified in the Project Gutenberg text; capitalization was modernized without changing the words.

quotes.quote: Do your work, and I shall know you. Do your work, and you shall reinforce yourself.
quotes.author: Ralph Waldo Emerson
quotes.authorBio: American essayist, lecturer, poet, and a leading figure of transcendentalism.

quotes.quote: One To-day is worth two To-morrows.
quotes.author: Benjamin Franklin
quotes.authorBio: American printer, writer, scientist, inventor, diplomat, and one of the Founding Fathers of the United States.
quotes.sourceTitle: The Way to Wealth, included with The Autobiography of Benjamin Franklin
quotes.verificationNote: Verified in the Project Gutenberg text; the original historical spelling and hyphenation are retained.
